# EBM0D { #paleobeasts.EBM0D }

```python
EBM0D(
    forcing,
    var_name='temperature',
    state_variables=None,
    diagnostic_variables=None,
    OLR=None,
    C=4,
    albedo=0.3,
)
```

Zero-dimensional energy balance model for global-mean surface temperature.

Evolves global-mean surface temperature T according to:

    C dT/dt = (1 - alpha) * S0/4 - OLR

where S0 is the solar constant supplied by ``forcing``, ``alpha`` is the
planetary albedo, and OLR is the outgoing longwave radiation.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name                 | Type                                 | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Default         |
|----------------------|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------|
| forcing              | pb.core.Forcing                      | Provides the solar constant S0 (W m⁻²) as a function of time.                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | _required_      |
| state_variables      | list of str                          | Names of the integrated state variables.  Default is ``['T']``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | `None`          |
| diagnostic_variables | list of str                          | Names of diagnostic quantities accumulated during integration. Default is ``['albedo', 'absorbed_SW', 'OLR', 'solar_incoming']``.                                                                                                                                                | `None`          |
| var_name             | str                                  | Label for the modeled quantity.  Default is ``'temperature'``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | `'temperature'` |
| OLR                  | callable or None                     | Outgoing longwave radiation.  Must have a compliant signature: ``(t)``, ``(t, state)``, or ``(t, state, model)`` with the first argument named ``t`` or ``time`` (see ``contracts/signal_model_contract.md``).  Default is Stefan-Boltzmann via ``OLR_func(pRad=650, ps=1000)``. | `None`          |
| C                    | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Heat capacity (W yr m⁻² K⁻¹).  If callable, must follow the parameter callable contract.  Default is 4.                                                                                                                                                                          | `4`             |
| albedo               | float or callable or pb.core.Forcing | Planetary albedo.  If callable, must follow the parameter callable contract.  Default is 0.3.                                                                                                                                                                                    | `0.3`           |

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

All parameters that support callables or Forcing objects are registered
in ``param_values`` (``'C'``, ``'albedo'``, ``'OLR'``) and resolved
at each timestep via ``get_param_value``.

This model uses ``uses_post_history = False``: state and diagnostics are
accumulated step-by-step inside ``dydt``.

## See also {.doc-section .doc-section-see-also}

EBM1DLat : Latitudinally-resolved 1D variant.
OLR_func : Default OLR callable factory.
albedo_func : Ice-albedo callable for use as the ``albedo`` parameter.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

.. code-block:: python

    import paleobeasts as pb
    from paleobeasts.signal_models.ebm import EBM0D

    forcing = pb.core.Forcing(lambda t: 1360.0)
    model = EBM0D(forcing=forcing)
    output = model.integrate(t_span=(0, 500), y0=[288.0], method='RK45')

With a time-varying albedo and custom OLR:

.. code-block:: python

    from paleobeasts.signal_models.ebm import EBM0D, albedo_func, OLR_func

    model = EBM0D(
        forcing=forcing,
        albedo=albedo_func,
        OLR=OLR_func(pRad=600),
    )

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [dydt](#paleobeasts.EBM0D.dydt) | Evaluate the right-hand side of the ODE at time t and state x. |

### dydt { #paleobeasts.EBM0D.dydt }

```python
EBM0D.dydt(t, x)
```

Evaluate the right-hand side of the ODE at time t and state x.

Called by the solver at each timestep.  As a side effect, appends the
current state to ``self.state_variables`` and appends each diagnostic
quantity to the corresponding list in ``self.diagnostic_variables``.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type         | Description                                                           | Default    |
|--------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| t      | float        | Current time.                                                         | _required_ |
| x      | array - like | Current state vector.  ``x[0]`` is the global-mean temperature T (K). | _required_ |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type          | Description                   |
|--------|---------------|-------------------------------|
| dydt   | list of float | Time-derivatives ``[dT/dt]``. |
